results.title: MEDUSA (weapon) - Wikipedia
excerpts:
Directed-energy non-lethal weapon

For the fictional weapon, see Mortal Engines .

**MEDUSA** ( **Mob Excess Deterrent Using Silent Audio** ) is a directed-energy non-lethal weapon designed by WaveBand Corporation in 2003-2004 for temporary personnel incapacitation. <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup> The weapon is based on the microwave auditory effect resulting in a strong sound sensation in the human head when it is subject to certain kinds of pulsed/modulated microwave radiation. The developers claimed that through the combination of pulse parameters and pulse power, it is possible to raise the auditory sensation to a “discomfort” level, deterring personnel from entering a protected perimeter or, if necessary, temporarily incapacitating particular individuals. <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup> In 2005, Sierra Nevada Corporation acquired WaveBand Corporation. <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup>

## Description

 [edit ]

According to the U.S.
Navy in 2004, the system would be "portable, low power, have a controllable radius of coverage, be able to switch from crowd to individual coverage, cause a temporarily incapacitating effect, have a low probability of fatality or permanent injury, cause no damage to property, and have a low probability of affecting friendly personnel". <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup> In addition to perimeter protection and crowd control , a proposed application of MEDUSA was "for use in systems to assist communication with hearing impaired persons". <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup>

## Fate

 [edit ]

The project received a positive initial evaluation from the Navy. However, Sierra Nevada Corporation had discontinued the project as of 2008, "possibly because it may have [been] shown to permanently damage human brain tissue". <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup>

## See also

 [edit ]

* Sonic weaponry

## References

 [edit ]

1. ^ <sup>_**a**_</sup> <sup>_**b**_</sup> ["Navy SBIR/STTR Search Database"](https://web.archive.
org/web/20080409063721/http://www.navysbirprogram.com/NavySearch/Summary/summary.aspx?pk=F5B07D68-1B19-4235-B140-950CE2E19D08) . NavySBIRProgram.com. 2004. Archived from [the original](http://www.navysbirprogram.com/NavySearch/Summary/summary.aspx?pk=F5B07D68-1B19-4235-B140-950CE2E19D08) on 2008-04-09.
2. **^** ["ACQUISITION OF WAVEBAND CORPORATION (WB) BY SIERRA NEVADA CORPORATION (SNC)"](https://web.archive.org/web/20121127045557/http://www.sncorp.com/press_more_info.php?id=350) . SNCorp.com. 2005-05-01. Archived from [the original](http://www.sncorp.com/press_more_info.php?id=350) on 2012-11-27.

results.title: US Navy MEDUSA Contract Summary: Remote Personnel ...
excerpts: This non-lethal weapon is based on the well established microwave auditory effect (MAE). MAE results in a strong sound sensation in the human head when it is irradiated with specifically selected microwave pulses of low energy.

results.title: Is archive.org safe and legal? I just downloaded an ePub ...
excerpts:
Is archive.org safe and legal? I just downloaded an ePub ...
While archive.org is a legitimate site, anyone can sign up and upload anything to it. The volume is too big for moderators to find everything

results.title: MEDUSA (Mob Excess Deterrent Using Silent Audio)
excerpts: The device - dubbed MEDUSA (Mob Excess Deterrent Using Silent Audio) - exploits the microwave audio effect, in which short microwave pulses rapidly heat tissue, causing a shockwave inside the skull that can be detected by the ears.
